titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fellowship of Helen Hadley Hall, Yale University, Records
Fellowship of Helen Hadley Hall, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday September 4, 2020 at 07:22

did.unittitle: Fellowship of Helen Hadley Hall, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records were created by the chairman of the Fellows of Helen Hadley Hall and include correspondence and memoranda, minutes, reports, directories, photographs of the fellows, notices, and other materials documenting the fellowship and the operation of Helen Hadley Hall. Included are records documenting the visit of Coretta Scott King to the campus in 1969 and Leona Baumgartner as fellow. Also included are records of Frances Blanshard concerning the Committee on the Status and Special Needs of Women at Yale, created by President Charles Seymour in 1945.

corpname.part: Yale University. Committee on the Status and Special Needs of Women at Yale

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 25-E (YRG 25-E), Records of departments, offices, programs, and projects of the Graduate School.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The records were transferred from Helen Hadley Hall, 1975. Subsequent gifts from Frances Blanshard, 1977 and 1979, and Claude-Anne Lopez, 1985.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Fellowship of Helen Hadley Hall, Yale University, Records (RU 9). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Helen Hadley Hall, a graduate women's residence, was erected on the Yale campus in 1958. In 1961, the Corporation established the Fellowship of Helen Hadley Hall, the first fellowship at Yale open to women. Chairmen of the fellows included Frances B. Blanshard, 1961-1962; Alice Elizabeth Chase, 1962-1970; and Claude-Anne Lopez, 1970-1973. The fellowship was discontinued in 1973, a number of years after women were admitted to residential college fellowships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records were created by the chairman of the Fellows of Helen Hadley Hall and include correspondence and memoranda, minutes, reports, directories, photographs of the fellows, notices, and other materials documenting the fellowship and the operation of Helen Hadley Hall. Included are records documenting the visit of Coretta Scott King to the campus in 1969 and Leona Baumgartner as fellow. Also included are records of Frances Blanshard concerning the Committee on the Status and Special Needs of Women at Yale, created by President Charles Seymour in 1945.

corpname.part: Yale University. Frances Blanshard Fellowship Fund

did.unittitle: Committee on Status and Special Needs of Women at Yale
List of foreign faculty and staff
Biography of Frances Blanshard, letter, reports
Additional lists, correspondence, printed materials relating to fellows
